ev.headline: Rivian Halts Software Update Rollout After Failures and Data Resets
ev.summary: Rivian has paused its software update rollout due to technical failures and data resets affecting vehicles. The halt marks a setback for the electric vehicle maker's software development efforts.

ev.headline: Tesla Launches Final Model S and Model X 'Signature Edition'
ev.summary: Tesla has announced final Signature Edition versions of the Model S and Model X, marking the end of these legacy models. The company is discontinuing these long-standing EV models.

ev.headline: Canada Invests C$10.6 Million in EV Charging Infrastructure Projects
ev.summary: Canada announced C$10.6 million investment in 14 EV charging infrastructure projects across the country as part of its Auto Strategy. The funding supports expanded charging access nationwide.

ev.headline: BYD's Chief Scientist Says Solid-State EV Batteries Reaching 'Critical Stage'
ev.summary: BYD's chief scientist Lian Yubo announced that solid-state EV batteries have entered a critical stage of development. BYD plans limited production starting in 2027 with full mass production around 2030.

ev.headline: Walmart Expands EV Charging Network by 50% in Two Months
ev.summary: Walmart has increased its DC fast charging stations by 50% in two months, reaching over 200 ports with 31 stations operational. The retailer plans thousands more chargers by 2030.

ev.headline: Kia Lowers EV Targets, Pivots to Hybrid Strategy for U.S. Market
ev.summary: Kia announced it will reduce EV targets and double its hybrid lineup, aiming for 25% hybrid sales in the U.S. by 2026. The company plans 14 global EVs by 2030 and new hybrid models.

ev.headline: Used EV Sales Hit Records as Lease Vehicles Return to Market
ev.summary: Used EV sales hit a wholesale record in Q1 2026 as off-lease vehicles flood the market. Experian expects EVs to comprise 15% of off-lease vehicles by end of 2026, up from 7.7% in Q1.

ev.headline: Toyota and Lexus Report Record Q1 2026 EV Sales
ev.summary: Toyota and Lexus achieved record EV sales in Q1 2026, boosted by updated models with NACS charging port access to Tesla Supercharger network. Both brands set all-time quarterly EV records.

ev.headline: Cox Automotive Reports EV Sales Stabilize at 5.8% Market Share in Q1 2026
ev.summary: EV market share stabilized at 5.8% in Q1 2026, unchanged from Q4 2025 after federal incentives expired. Cox Automotive expects recovery driven by affordability and new model launches.

ev.headline: Global EV Sales Reach 19.9% Market Share, Driven by China Growth
ev.summary: Global EV sales increased 25% in 2024 to 17.8 million units, achieving 19.9% market share. Growth was driven by strong momentum in China and accelerating PHEV adoption.

ev.headline: Rimac Technology to Build BMW i7 Battery Packs Using Gen6 Cylindrical Cells
ev.summary: Rimac Technology will manufacture battery packs for BMW's flagship i7 sedan using new Gen6 cylindrical cells, marking first commercial deployment. The partnership supports BMW's Neue Klasse technology rollout.

ev.headline: Elektros Advances Patented EV Charging Platform Amid Rising Fuel Costs
ev.summary: Elektros is advancing a patented multiplug fast-charging platform designed to reduce charge times and improve efficiency. Rising gasoline prices are accelerating demand for fast charging solutions.

ev.headline: Tesla Begins Rolling Out Full Self-Driving to Customers in Netherlands
ev.summary: Tesla has begun rolling out its Full Self-Driving (FSD) capability to the first customers in the Netherlands. The rollout marks a milestone for Tesla's autonomous driving technology expansion in Europe.

ev.headline: BYD Denza Z9GT Shooting Brake Launches in Europe with 10-Minute Charging
ev.summary: BYD's Denza brand launched the premium Z9GT shooting brake in Europe with 10-minute rapid charging capability. Vehicle features BYD's Flash Charging system and second-generation Blade Battery.

ev.headline: Kia Achieves 100,000 EV Sales in UK, First Korean Brand to Hit Milestone
ev.summary: Kia sold its 100,000th electric vehicle in the UK, becoming the first Korean brand to reach this milestone. The Niro EV accounted for 51,000 of those sales before discontinuation.

ev.headline: Nissan NX8 Electrified SUV Launches in China with 8,500 Orders in 30 Minutes
ev.summary: Nissan's NX8 electrified SUV launched in China with BEV and REEV powertrains, booking close to 8,500 orders within 30 minutes. Vehicle features 800V fast charging capability.

ev.headline: Volkswagen Updates ID.4 and ID.5 with Larger Batteries and V2L Capability
ev.summary: Volkswagen refreshed its ID.4 and ID.5 lineup with bigger batteries, Vehicle-to-Load capability, and improved safety features. UK pricing starts from £36,995 for the updated models.

ev.headline: Automakers Face $60 Billion in EV Strategy Writedowns Since 2025
ev.summary: Major automakers have announced over $60 billion in collective writedowns for EV investments since 2025. Stellantis ($26.3B), Ford ($19.5B), Honda ($20.7B) and GM ($6B) cite disappointing demand.

ev.headline: Trump Administration Proposes 100% Domestic Content for EV Charger Federal Funding
ev.summary: The Trump administration proposed raising domestic content requirements from 55% to 100% for EV chargers receiving federal funding. The move aims to boost domestic manufacturing but critics say it may hinder infrastructure rollout.

ev.headline: Sodium-Ion EV Battery Breakthrough Achieves 450km Range and 11-Minute Charging
ev.summary: Beijing Automotive Group (BAIC) announced a breakthrough sodium-ion battery with 170Wh/kg energy density. CATL's sodium-ion batteries achieve 175Wh/kg, rivaling LFP battery performance.

ev.headline: Factorial Energy Solid-State Battery Achieves 745-Mile Range in Real-World Test
ev.summary: Factorial Energy's solid-state battery powered a Mercedes test vehicle to 745 miles on a single charge. The company plans to bring its Solstice platform to market by 2027.

ev.headline: California Launches $200M EV Incentive Program to Offset Lost Federal Tax Credits
ev.summary: California is launching a $200 million EV incentive program designed to offset the loss of the federal $7,500 tax credit. The state remains committed to zero-emission vehicle goals despite federal policy changes.

ev.headline: Chery Rhino Solid-State Battery Achieves 600Wh/kg Energy Density
ev.summary: Chinese automaker Chery announced its Rhino series solid-state battery with 600Wh/kg energy density enabling 800-mile range. The company plans vehicle testing next year starting with the Exeed ES8.

ev.headline: Hyundai Ioniq 9 Three-Row EV Offers 335-Mile Range and Fast Charging
ev.summary: Hyundai's 2026 Ioniq 9 three-row EV debuted with 335 miles of range and lightning-fast charging times. The vehicle balances driving dynamics, efficiency and spacious interior for family transport.

ev.headline: BMW iX3 Confirmed for U.S. Market Summer 2026 with 400-Mile Range
ev.summary: BMW's iX3 electric midsize luxury SUV has been confirmed for U.S. sale starting summer 2026. The 800-volt vehicle charges 10-80% in 21 minutes and delivers up to 400 miles of range.

energy.headline: Colorado Net Metering Policy Remains Strong for Solar Homeowners in 2026
energy.summary: Colorado continues offering one of the strongest net metering policies in the country, with Xcel Energy crediting solar customers at full retail rates while also providing battery storage incentives of $100 annually for five years.

energy.headline: U.S. Battery Energy Storage Deployments Jump 29% in 2025 to Exceed 28 GW/57 GWh
energy.summary: U.S. residential storage installations surged 51% year-over-year in 2025, with virtual power plant programs in Massachusetts, Texas, Arizona, and Illinois driving deployment growth despite federal policy challenges.

energy.headline: U.S. Installs Record 57.6 GWh of Energy Storage in 2025
energy.summary: The U.S. energy storage industry reached record-breaking capacity additions in 2025, with 137 GWh of utility-scale storage now installed nationally and over 600 GWh expected by 2030.

energy.headline: Solar Installs Surged 205% Before Tax Credit Cut and Reshaped Home Electrification Market
energy.summary: Homeowners rushed to install solar before federal tax credits ended December 31, 2025, driving a 205% surge in demand with installers reaching capacity by October while prices remained surprisingly stable.

energy.headline: Batteries Become the New Home Solar as Net Metering Evolves and Energy Prices Soar
energy.summary: California's NEM 3.0 policy change has reduced solar export compensation by up to 75%, shifting homeowner strategy toward pairing solar with battery storage for self-consumption rather than grid export.

energy.headline: States Consider 'New Major Revisions' to Net Metering Policies in Q1 2025
energy.summary: A quarterly report from NC Clean Energy Technology Center found that 47 states took distributed solar policy actions in Q1 2025, with about one-third of states offering alternatives to traditional net metering or making significant revisions.

energy.headline: California Residents Increasingly Pairing Battery Storage with Solar Installations
energy.summary: California solar installations paired with battery storage surged from just 20% in October 2023 to over 50% in April 2024, driven by changes to the state's net metering compensation structure.

energy.headline: US Residential Solar Enters Post-Incentive Era After ITC Expiry Surge
energy.summary: After the One Big Beautiful Bill Act eliminated the 30% residential solar tax credit at year-end 2025, homeowners shifted toward third-party ownership models like leases and PPAs to retain incentive eligibility through 2028.

energy.headline: Net Metering vs. Batteries in 2026: Which Saves You More on Solar?
energy.summary: With net metering compensation changing in multiple states and residential battery costs ranging from $1,100-$1,300 per kWh before incentives, homeowners face critical decisions on whether to pursue net metering or pair solar with storage.

energy.headline: Dozens of States Consider DIY Plug-in Solar Panel Legislation
energy.summary: More than two dozen states are advancing legislation allowing households to use simple plug-in solar arrays, with Maine's bill nearing the governor's desk as energy affordability concerns drive policy changes.

energy.headline: Nevada Utilities File Net Metering Successor Tariffs with Regulators
energy.summary: Nevada Power and Sierra Power filed tariffs to move from monthly netting to 15-minute netting for rooftop solar systems under 25 kW, representing continued policy evolution across the state.

energy.headline: Solar Energy Storage Systems Gain Growing Attention Amid Rising Electricity Prices
energy.summary: Home battery storage adoption surged as homeowners recognize battery systems can function as energy arbitrage devices, charging when electricity is cheap and discharging during expensive peak demand hours.

energy.headline: Historic Policy Upheaval Reveals Mature, Competitive Home Energy Market in 2025
energy.summary: Despite unprecedented demand surge before year-end 2025 tax credit deadline, solar pricing remained stable with median costs at $2.50/W in Q4, suggesting market maturity over panic pricing.

energy.headline: Federal Energy Storage Deployments Expected to Reach 600+ GWh by 2030
energy.summary: Despite federal policy actions targeting clean energy, energy storage deployments grew 30% in 2025 and are projected to reach over 600 GWh by 2030, with utility-scale installations leading growth.

energy.headline: South Carolina Updates Net Metering Policies for 2025
energy.summary: South Carolina's net metering framework has evolved with electric cooperatives offering varying approaches from near-retail credit rates to net billing at wholesale rates.

energy.headline: Homeowners Shift Toward Whole-Home Electrification with Integrated Solar and Battery Systems
energy.summary: The market is evolving from single-product purchases to coordinated whole-home energy systems combining solar, battery storage, EV charging, and heat pumps for cost control and resilience.

energy.headline: Consumer's Guide to Rooftop Solar and Home Energy Storage Released
energy.summary: Researchers from Pacific Northwest National Laboratory published an open-access guide analyzing utility rates, net metering policies, installation costs, and financial incentives for residential solar and battery storage systems.

energy.headline: A New Inflection Point: America Ready for Whole-Home Electrification
energy.summary: Industry leaders indicate that the technical, policy, and financial pieces are now in place for widespread whole-home electrification integrating solar, batteries, EVs, and heat pumps into unified systems.

energy.headline: Virtual Power Plants Demonstrate Grid Support Potential in California Test
energy.summary: Over 100,000 California homes in a Sunrun virtual power plant test dispatched 535 megawatts of electricity to the grid in July 2025, equivalent to half of San Francisco's energy demand.

energy.headline: Energy Storage Market Outlook: Texas Overtakes California as Largest Utility-Scale Storage Market
energy.summary: While Texas became the largest utility-scale energy storage market by gigawatts deployed in 2025, California remains the runaway leader in cumulative storage deployments with 59.8 GWh deployed.

energy.headline: Solar Panel Installation Costs Fall to All-Time Low Before Tax Credit Deadline
energy.summary: In Q3 2025, solar installer median quoted prices fell to an all-time low of $2.47/W as contractors raced to sign contracts before the December 31 federal tax credit deadline.

energy.headline: Residential Battery Attachment Rates Decline as Homeowners Prioritize Solar Tax Credits
energy.summary: Home battery attachment to residential solar systems fell from 41% to 38% in H2 2025, with California and Texas seeing steeper declines as homeowners prioritized securing solar incentives before deadline.

energy.headline: Net Metering Is One of the Best Solar Incentives, But Changes Are Coming
energy.summary: Four states in 2023 approved amendments significantly lowering net metering compensation, with Hawaii pioneering the shift to net billing and California's NEM 3.0 reducing export values by 75%.

energy.headline: Heat Pump Installation Surge Contributes to Home Electrification Growth
energy.summary: As part of broader home electrification trends, heat pump installations are increasing alongside solar and battery storage as homeowners pursue comprehensive energy efficiency upgrades.

energy.headline: 34 States Plus D.C. and Puerto Rico Have Mandatory Net Metering Rules
energy.summary: Current net metering policies remain in place across 34 states plus Washington D.C. and Puerto Rico, though many are undergoing revisions to compensation structures and program eligibility.

energy.headline: Plug-in Solar Panel Technology Gains Momentum Across Six States
energy.summary: Maine is among six states advancing legislation for simple plug-in solar panel systems, driven by energy affordability concerns and growing consumer interest in DIY clean energy solutions.

v2x.headline: Puget Sound Energy Launches First Washington State V2H Pilot Program
v2x.summary: Washington utility Puget Sound Energy began testing vehicle-to-home charging technology in March 2026, marking the first bidirectional EV charging pilot in the state, partnering with Ford, Kia, Wallbox and ChargeScape.

v2x.headline: Renault Group Launches Plug Inn: Unified V2G Charging Ecosystem
v2x.summary: Renault Group rebrands its charging solutions under 'Plug Inn,' consolidating bidirectional charging and V2G services across Europe with new powerbox technology and intelligent charging management.

v2x.headline: ChargePoint Introduces Bidirectional AC Architecture for Maximum-Speed EV Charging
v2x.summary: ChargePoint unveils new bidirectional AC charging architecture delivering speeds up to double typical Level 2 chargers, with models arriving in Europe this summer and North America end of 2025.

v2x.headline: Nature Study: Decentralized EV Charging Enables Large-Scale Solar Integration in Tropical Cities
v2x.summary: Research published in Nature Communications demonstrates how V2G technology helps balance photovoltaic generation fluctuations in tropical climates, enabling greater renewable energy deployment.

v2x.headline: BMW Neue Klasse Rolls Out V2G with Up to 720 Euro Annual Bonus
v2x.summary: BMW launched bidirectional V2G technology across its Neue Klasse lineup starting with the iX3, offering customers up to 720 euros annually for grid services through partnership with E.ON.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging Reaches 2026 Mainstream Adoption Milestone
v2x.summary: Major automakers including Ford, Tesla, GM, and Mitsubishi now offer bidirectional charging capabilities with V2H becoming standard and V2G expanding across markets.

v2x.headline: Enphase Energy Unveils IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for H2 2026 Launch
v2x.summary: Enphase announced its IQ Bidirectional EV Charger with AI-powered energy management and black-start capability for V2H and V2G, expected available second half of 2026.

v2x.headline: V2H Technology Emerges as Practical Bidirectional Solution for 2026 Australian Homes
v2x.summary: Analysis shows vehicle-to-home charging is arriving sooner than V2G for Australian homeowners, offering immediate benefits without regulatory barriers or utility participation requirements.

v2x.headline: NREL Research: V2G Programs May Extend EV Battery Lifespan
v2x.summary: National Renewable Energy Laboratory study finds EV batteries may last longer when participating in V2G programs, as lower state-of-charge during parked periods reduces battery degradation.

v2x.headline: GM Energy V2H Kit Now Available with Early Adopter Discounts
v2x.summary: General Motors offers its V2H Enablement Kit with promotional pricing through April 30, 2026, allowing GM EV owners to unlock bidirectional charging through the PowerShift Charger.

v2x.headline: Ford F-150 Lightning Leads US V2H Market as First High-Volume Bidirectional EV
v2x.summary: Ford's F-150 Lightning with Charge Station Pro capability continues to set the standard for vehicle-to-home applications, powering homes for up to three days during outages.

v2x.headline: Tesla Cybertruck V2H Now Active: 11.5kW Home Backup Power Available
v2x.summary: Tesla's Cybertruck offers V2H capability through its Powershare system with 11.5kW output, featuring internal bidirectional inverter for AC power conversion at the charge port.

v2x.headline: Massachusetts Launches Statewide V2X Pilot Deploying 100 Bidirectional Chargers
v2x.summary: Massachusetts Clean Energy Center backs two-year initiative to deploy 100 bidirectional chargers across homes, school buses, municipal and commercial fleet participants.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid Conference Set for April 2026 in Germany
v2x.summary: The Vehicle-to-Grid Conference (April 15-16, 2026, Münster) brings together automotive, energy, and technology leaders to discuss grid-connected mobility applications and business models.

v2x.headline: Australian Bidirectional Chargers Approved for Network Connection
v2x.summary: Ausgrid approves Numbat V2G charger for NSW connection, supporting both CHAdeMO and CCS2 standards with pricing expected around A$10,000 plus GST.

v2x.headline: Lucid Motors Plans V2H Launch in 'Not-Too-Distant Future'
v2x.summary: Lucid Motors interim CEO Marc Winterhoff announced upcoming vehicle-to-home charging capability, positioning the company to join other premium EV makers offering bidirectional technology.

v2x.headline: Rivian Announces 2026 V2H Launch for Premium EV Lineup
v2x.summary: Premium EV maker Rivian plans to introduce vehicle-to-home charging in 2026, though specific development updates have not been provided since initial announcement.

v2x.headline: PG&E V2X Pilot Programs Offer Incentives for Bidirectional Charger Adoption
v2x.summary: Pacific Gas and Electric runs Vehicle-to-Everything pilot programs providing incentives for customers to access bidirectional charging technology with time-of-use optimization.

v2x.headline: Wallbox Quasar 2 Available for Kia EV9 Owners with V2H/V2G Support
v2x.summary: Wallbox announces Quasar 2 bidirectional charger availability for Kia EV9 owners, offering up to 11.5kW power with V2H backup and V2G capabilities.

v2x.headline: DOE EVGrid Assist Initiative Supports Vehicle-Grid Integration Planning
v2x.summary: Department of Energy's EVGrid Assist provides resources and collaborative forums to help stakeholders implement vehicle grid integration solutions benefiting all EV users.

v2x.headline: Managed Charging Scales as Utilities Deploy V1G Grid Integration Solutions
v2x.summary: Utilities increasingly deploy managed charging (V1G) to shift load from peak hours, with active programs moving beyond pilot phase and scaling for wider grid benefits.

v2x.headline: BYD Dolphin Brings V2G to UK Market with Integrated Bundled Offering
v2x.summary: Chinese EV maker BYD launches bidirectional charging in UK with Dolphin model, pairing vehicle with Zaptec wallbox and Octopus Energy tariff for complete V2G solution.

v2x.headline: Standards-Based ISO 15118-20 V2G Coming in 2026 for Interoperable Charging
v2x.summary: ISO 15118-20 standard enabling interoperable V2G across vehicles and chargers expected to drive mass adoption and cost reductions in 2026 and beyond.

v2x.headline: V2G Economics: EV Batteries Could Provide 32-62 TWh Capacity by 2050
v2x.summary: NREL study predicts EV batteries could meet global short-term storage needs by 2030 even with low participation rates, transforming grid resilience economics.
